Ideate and strategise a solution for the following problem.

PROBLEM STATEMENT
As an AI agent using the mmr CLI you will sometimes want to inspect the messages of the previous session. 'mmr' defaults to the 'current' session, meaning that running mmr by default will get the agent the messages of the session they are already in. 

PROPOSALS
- It is useful to be able to pull 'messages' from a session by reverse index - i.e. I want to see the messages of the previous session (i.e. 'mmr messages --from-index -1 --to-index -1)
- It may be useful to be able to pull 'messages' from a range of sessions - i.e. I want to see the messages of the 2 sessions before this one (i.e. 'mmr messages --from-index -2 --to-index -1)

DISCUSS
- can we make UX of the CLI more ergonomic and easier to understand?

730	pub async fn run_cli(cli: Cli) -> Result<String> {
731	    let source_filter = effective_source(cli.source);
732	    if let Commands::Note { text } = &cli.command {
733	        return serialize(&note_response(text.clone())?, cli.pretty);
734	    }
735	    if let Commands::Rg(args) = &cli.command {
736	        return rg_output(args, source_filter, cli.pretty);
737	    }
738	    if let Commands::Search(args) = &cli.command {
739	        if args.line {
740	            bail!("--line is only supported for `mmr rg`");
741	        }
742	        return serialize(&search_response(args, source_filter)?, cli.pretty);
743	    }
744	    if let Commands::Dream(args) = &cli.command {
745	        return serialize(&dream_response(args)?, cli.pretty);
746	    }
747	    if let Commands::Import(args) = &cli.command {
748	        return serialize(&import_response(args, source_filter)?, cli.pretty);
749	    }
750	    if let Commands::Redact(args) = &cli.command {
751	        return serialize(&redact_response(args, source_filter)?, cli.pretty);
752	    }
753	    if let Commands::Sync(args) = &cli.command {
754	        return serialize(&sync_response(args, source_filter)?, cli.pretty);
755	    }
756	    if let Commands::Status(args) = &cli.command {
757	        return serialize(&status_response(args, source_filter)?, cli.pretty);
758	    }
759	    if let Commands::DbInfo {
760	        project,
761	        smoke_event,
762	    } = &cli.command
763	    {
764	        return serialize(
765	            &db_info_response(project.clone(), *smoke_event)?,
766	            cli.pretty,
767	        );
768	    }
769	    if let Commands::Teleport(args) = &cli.command {
770	        return teleport_command_response(args, source_filter, cli.pretty);
771	    }
772	
773	    let service = QueryService::load()?;
774	
775	    let response = match cli.command {
776	        Commands::Projects {
777	            limit,
778	            offset,
779	            sort_by,
780	            order,
781	        } => serialize(
782	            &service.projects(
783	                source_filter,
784	                Some(limit),
785	                offset,
786	                SortOptions::new(sort_by, order),
787	            ),
788	            cli.pretty,
789	        )?,
790	        Commands::Sessions {
791	            project,
792	            all,
793	            limit,
794	            offset,
795	            sort_by,
796	            order,
797	        } => serialize(
798	            &service.sessions(
799	                effective_project_scope(project, all).as_deref(),
800	                source_filter,
801	                Some(limit),
802	                offset,
803	                SortOptions::new(sort_by, order),
804	            )?,
805	            cli.pretty,
806	        )?,
807	        Commands::Messages {
808	            session,
809	            project,
810	            all,
811	            latest,
812	            from_message_index,
813	            to_message_index,
814	            limit,
815	            offset,
816	            sort_by,
817	            order,
818	        } => {
819	            let message_index_range =
820	                validate_message_index_range(from_message_index, to_message_index)?;
821	            // When a session ID is provided without an explicit project,
822	            // skip cwd auto-discovery and search all projects instead.
823	            let project_scope = if session.is_some() && project.is_none() {
824	                if source_filter.is_none() {
825	                    eprintln!(
826	                        "hint: searching all sources for session; pass --source to narrow the search"
827	                    );
828	                }
829	                None
830	            } else {
831	                effective_project_scope(project.clone(), all)
832	            };
833	
834	            let mut response = if let Some(latest) = latest {
835	                service.latest_session_messages(
836	                    session.as_deref(),
837	                    project_scope.as_deref(),
838	                    source_filter,
839	                    latest.get(),
840	                    message_index_range,
841	                )?
842	            } else {
843	                service.messages(
844	                    session.as_deref(),
845	                    project_scope.as_deref(),
846	                    source_filter,
847	                    MessageQueryOptions::new(Some(limit), offset, SortOptions::new(sort_by, order))
848	                        .with_message_index_range(message_index_range),
849	                )?
850	            };
851	            if latest.is_none() && response.next_page {
852	                response.next_command = Some(build_next_messages_command(
853	                    cli.source,
854	                    cli.pretty,
855	                    session.as_deref(),
856	                    project.as_deref(),
857	                    all,
858	                    message_index_range,
859	                    limit,
860	                    response.next_offset as usize,
861	                    sort_by,
862	                    order,
863	                ));
864	            }
865	            serialize(&response, cli.pretty)?
866	        }
867	        Commands::Export {
868	            project,
869	            format,
870	            output_dir,
871	        } => {
872	            if format == ExportFormatArg::Tree {
873	                let response = export_tree_response(project, output_dir, source_filter)?;
874	                serialize(&response, cli.pretty)?
875	            } else {
876	                let sort = SortOptions::new(SortBy::Timestamp, SortOrder::Asc);
877	                if let Some(proj) = project {
878	                    let response = service.messages(
879	                        None,
880	                        Some(proj.as_str()),
881	                        source_filter,
882	                        MessageQueryOptions::new(None, 0, sort),
883	                    )?;
884	                    serialize(&response, cli.pretty)?
885	                } else {
886	                    let (codex_path, claude_name) =
887	                        resolve_project_from_cwd().context("could not get current directory")?;
888	                    let cursor_name = claude_name.clone();
889	                    let mut messages: Vec<ApiMessage> = Vec::new();
890	                    if source_filter.is_none() || source_filter == Some(SourceFilter::Codex) {
891	                        let codex = service.messages(
892	                            None,
893	                            Some(&codex_path),
894	                            Some(SourceFilter::Codex),
895	                            MessageQueryOptions::new(None, 0, sort),
896	                        )?;
897	                        messages.extend(codex.messages);
898	                    }
899	                    if source_filter.is_none() || source_filter == Some(SourceFilter::Claude) {
900	                        let claude = service.messages(
901	                            None,
902	                            Some(&claude_name),
903	                            Some(SourceFilter::Claude),
904	                            MessageQueryOptions::new(None, 0, sort),
905	                        )?;
906	                        messages.extend(claude.messages);
907	                    }
908	                    if source_filter.is_none() || source_filter == Some(SourceFilter::Cursor) {
909	                        let cursor = service.messages(
910	                            None,
911	                            Some(&cursor_name),
912	                            Some(SourceFilter::Cursor),
913	                            MessageQueryOptions::new(None, 0, sort),
914	                        )?;
915	                        messages.extend(cursor.messages);
916	                    }
917	                    if source_filter.is_none() || source_filter == Some(SourceFilter::Grok) {
918	                        let grok = service.messages(
919	                            None,
920	                            Some(&codex_path),
921	                            Some(SourceFilter::Grok),
922	                            MessageQueryOptions::new(None, 0, sort),
923	                        )?;
924	                        messages.extend(grok.messages);
925	                    }
926	                    if source_filter.is_none() || source_filter == Some(SourceFilter::Pi) {
927	                        let pi = service.messages(
928	                            None,
929	                            Some(&codex_path),
930	                            Some(SourceFilter::Pi),
931	                            MessageQueryOptions::new(None, 0, sort),
932	                        )?;
933	                        messages.extend(pi.messages);
934	                    }
935	                    messages.sort_by(|a, b| {
936	                        a.timestamp
937	                            .cmp(&b.timestamp)
938	                            .then_with(|| a.session_id.cmp(&b.session_id))
939	                    });
940	                    let total = messages.len() as i64;
941	                    let response = ApiMessagesResponse {
942	                        messages,
943	                        total_messages: total,
944	                        next_page: false,
945	                        next_offset: total,
946	                        next_command: None,
947	                    };
948	                    serialize(&response, cli.pretty)?
949	                }
950	            }
951	        }
952	        Commands::Summary(remember) | Commands::Remember(remember) => {
953	            remember_command_response(&service, remember, source_filter, cli.pretty).await?
954	        }
955	        Commands::Link => serialize(&link_response(source_filter)?, cli.pretty)?,
956	        Commands::Import(args) => serialize(&import_response(&args, source_filter)?, cli.pretty)?,
957	        Commands::Note { text } => serialize(&note_response(text)?, cli.pretty)?,
958	        Commands::Rg(args) => rg_output(&args, source_filter, cli.pretty)?,
959	        Commands::Search(args) => {
960	            if args.line {
961	                bail!("--line is only supported for `mmr rg`");
962	            }
963	            serialize(&search_response(&args, source_filter)?, cli.pretty)?
964	        }
965	        Commands::Dream(args) => serialize(&dream_response(&args)?, cli.pretty)?,
966	        Commands::Redact(args) => serialize(&redact_response(&args, source_filter)?, cli.pretty)?,
967	        Commands::Sync(args) => serialize(&sync_response(&args, source_filter)?, cli.pretty)?,
968	        Commands::Status(args) => serialize(&status_response(&args, source_filter)?, cli.pretty)?,
969	        Commands::DbInfo {
970	            project,
971	            smoke_event,
972	        } => serialize(&db_info_response(project, smoke_event)?, cli.pretty)?,
973	        Commands::Teleport(_) => unreachable!("teleport handled before QueryService load"),
974	    };
975	
976	    Ok(response)
977	}

1347	fn teleport_success_or_unsupported<T: Serialize>(response: T, pretty: bool) -> Result<String> {
1348	    let status = serde_json::to_value(&response).ok().and_then(|value| {
1349	        value
1350	            .get("status")
1351	            .and_then(|status| status.as_str())
1352	            .map(str::to_string)
1353	    });
1354	    let json = serialize(&response, pretty)?;
1355	    if status.as_deref() == Some("unsupported") {
1356	        let stderr = response_message(&response).unwrap_or_else(|| {
1357	            "teleport: requested cross-agent transform is not supported".to_string()
1358	        });
1359	        return Err(anyhow::Error::new(CliFailure::new(3, json, stderr)));
1360	    }
1361	    Ok(json)
1362	}

1364	fn response_message<T: Serialize>(response: &T) -> Option<String> {
1365	    serde_json::to_value(response)
1366	        .ok()?
1367	        .get("message")?
1368	        .as_str()
1369	        .map(str::to_string)
1370	}

1385	    }

1396	fn note_response(text: Vec<String>) -> Result<NoteResponse> {
1397	    let mut store = Store::open_default()?;
1398	    let cwd = std::env::current_dir().context("current_dir")?;
1399	    let project = store.project_by_path(&cwd)?.ok_or_else(|| {
1400	        anyhow::anyhow!("current project is not linked; run `mmr link` before adding notes")
1401	    })?;
1402	    let content = read_note_text(text)?;
1403	    let timestamp = now_rfc3339()?;
1404	    let note_identity = content_hash(&format!("{timestamp}:{content}"));
1405	    let source_event_id = format!("note:{note_identity}");
1406	    let event = NewEvent::new(
1407	        "note",
1408	        "notes",
1409	        "note",
1410	        "user",
1411	        timestamp.clone(),
1412	        content,
1413	        "note-v1",
1414	    )
1415	    .with_source_event_id(source_event_id)
1416	    .with_raw_local_ref(format!("note://notes/{note_identity}"));
1417	    let (event, search_document) = store.insert_event_with_search_document(&project.id, &event)?;
1418	
1419	    Ok(NoteResponse {
1420	        project_id: project.id,
1421	        event_id: event.id,
1422	        source: event.source,
1423	        citation: search_document.citation,
1424	    })
1425	}

1427	fn read_note_text(text: Vec<String>) -> Result<String> {
1428	    let note = if text.is_empty() {
1429	        let mut buffer = String::new();
1430	        std::io::stdin()
1431	            .read_to_string(&mut buffer)
1432	            .context("read note from stdin")?;
1433	        buffer
1434	    } else {
1435	        text.join(" ")
1436	    };
1437	    let note = note.trim_matches(['\n', '\r']).trim().to_string();
1438	    if note.is_empty() {
1439	        bail!("note text is empty");
1440	    }
1441	    Ok(note)
1442	}

1591	fn link_response(source_filter: Option<SourceFilter>) -> Result<LinkResponse> {
1592	    let mut store = Store::open_default()?;
1593	    let info = store.info()?;
1594	    let cwd = std::env::current_dir().context("current_dir")?;
1595	    let already_linked = store.project_by_path(&cwd)?.is_some();
1596	    let project = store.ensure_project_link(&cwd)?;
1597	    let remote = remote_for_status()?;
1598	    let remote_auth_ok = remote.summary().auth_status == "ok";
1599	    let hydration = if remote_auth_ok {
1600	        hydrate_project(&mut store, &project, &remote)?
1601	    } else {
1602	        HydrationReport {
1603	            remote_events: 0,
1604	            inserted_events: 0,
1605	            existing_events: 0,
1606	            remote_learned_memory: 0,
1607	            inserted_learned_memory: 0,
1608	            existing_learned_memory: 0,
1609	        }
1610	    };
1611	    let imports = reconcile_default_sources(&mut store, &project, source_filter)?;
1612	    let rebuilt_search_documents = rebuild_search_documents(&store, &project, source_filter)?;
1613	    let sync = if remote_auth_ok {
1614	        sync_project(
1615	            &mut store,
1616	            &project,
1617	            &remote,
1618	            source_filter_name(source_filter),
1619	        )?
1620	    } else {
1621	        remote_pending_sync_report(&store, &project, &remote, source_filter)?
1622	    };
1623	    let status = status_snapshot(&store, Some(&project), Some(&remote))?;
1624	    Ok(LinkResponse {
1625	        command: "link".to_string(),
1626	        already_linked,
1627	        store: StoreStatus {
1628	            schema_version: info.schema_version,
1629	        },
1630	        project: project_status(&project),
1631	        remote: sync.remote.clone(),
1632	        hydration,
1633	        imports,
1634	        rebuilt_search_documents,
1635	        sync,
1636	        status,
1637	    })
1638	}

1644	    source_filter: Option<SourceFilter>,
1645	) -> Result<SyncReport> {
1646	    let events = store.events_for_project(&project.id, source_filter_name(source_filter), None)?;
1647	    Ok(SyncReport {
1648	        status: "remote_pending".to_string(),

1	# Session Lookup Invariants
2	
3	This document specifies the invariant behavior when looking up messages by session ID.
4	
5	## Invariant: `--session` bypasses cwd project auto-discovery
6	
7	When `mmr messages --session <ID>` is called **without** an explicit `--project`, the command must search across **all projects** — the default cwd-based project auto-discovery is not applied.
8	
9	### Rationale
10	
11	A session ID is globally unique. The caller already knows which session they want; scoping the search to the cwd project would silently return zero results when the session belongs to a different project. This is confusing and defeats the purpose of the lookup.
12	
13	### Rules
14	
15	| Flags provided               | Project scope          | Hint printed |
16	| ----------------------------- | ---------------------- | ------------ |
17	| `--session`                   | All projects (no cwd)  | Yes (`--source`) |
18	| `--session --source X`        | All projects (no cwd)  | No           |
19	| `--session --project P`       | Explicit project `P`   | No           |
20	| (no `--session`)              | cwd auto-discovery     | No           |
21	
22	### Hint message
23	
24	When `--session` is provided without `--source`, a hint is printed to stderr:
25	
26	```
27	hint: searching all sources for session; pass --source to narrow the search
28	```
29	
30	This nudges the caller toward a faster, more targeted lookup without blocking the operation.
31	
32	### Contract tests
33	
34	Covered by integration tests in `tests/cli_contract.rs`:
35	
36	- `messages_session_without_project_searches_all_projects` — session found even when cwd points to a different project
37	- `messages_session_without_project_or_source_prints_hint` — hint appears on stderr
38	- `messages_session_with_source_does_not_print_hint` — hint suppressed when `--source` provided
39	- `messages_session_with_explicit_project_uses_project_scope` — explicit `--project` still applies project scoping

1	# Messages Command
2	
3	## `--latest`
4	
5	`mmr messages --latest` selects the latest session in the current scope and returns only the latest message from that session.
6	
7	`mmr messages --latest <N>` selects the latest session in the current scope and returns the latest `N` messages from that session.
8	
9	The returned latest-session window is ordered chronologically. Existing scope filters still apply, including `--source`, `--project`, `--all`, and `--session`.
10	
11	## `--from-message-index` and `--to-message-index`
12	
13	`mmr messages --from-message-index <N>` starts the result window at zero-based message index `N` after source, project, session, sort, and latest-session filtering.
14	
15	`mmr messages --to-message-index <N>` stops the result window before zero-based message index `N`.
16	
17	When both flags are supplied, `--from-message-index` is inclusive and `--to-message-index` is exclusive. The returned window remains ordered according to the selected message ordering. `total_messages` continues to report the full scoped message count before applying the message-index window.

The CLI calls `QueryService::messages` once per source when using cwd, then merges and sorts by timestamp (asc).
65	- `mmr export --project <path>` passes the project to a single `messages` call (all sources unless `--source` is set). Reuses existing `ApiMessagesResponse`; no new response type.

74	
75	## Remember command and `--instructions` system prompt architecture
76	
77	The `remember` command sends session transcripts to the backend selected with `--agent` (`cursor`, `codex`, or `gemini`; default `cursor` with `composer-2-fast` when `--model` is omitted). For each backend, the memory flow uses a system prompt composed of two parts:
78	
79	1. **Base instruction** (`MEMORY_AGENT_BASE_INSTRUCTION` in `src/agent/ai.rs`): Always present. Contains only the agent's identity ("You are a Memory Agent") and the input format description. Must **never** contain output-directing language (e.g. "continuity brief", "sole purpose", output quality directives).
80	
81	2. **Output instruction** (appended after the base):
82	   - **Without `--instructions`**: `MEMORY_AGENT_DEFAULT_OUTPUT_INSTRUCTION` is used — includes `## Purpose`, `## Output Format`, `## Rules`, and `### Resume Instructions` sections.
83	   - **With `--instructions <text>`**: The custom text **replaces** the entire default output instruction. The base (identity + input format) is preserved, but all output-directing sections are replaced by the user's text.
84	
85	This separation ensures `--instructions` has full control over how the agent processes transcripts and formats its response, while preserving the agent's awareness of its role and input structure.
86	
87	The user prompt is neutral ("Analyze the following AI coding session transcript(s).") and does not prescribe an output format, so the system instruction has sole authority over output behavior.
88	
89	Environment: **Gemini** — `GOOGLE_API_KEY` or `GEMINI_API_KEY`; optional `GEMINI_API_BASE_URL` (integration tests use a mock server). **Codex** — Codex CLI auth as configured for `codex exec`. **Cursor** — `CURSOR_API_KEY` and the `agent` CLI on `PATH`.

1	# ADR-002: CWD-Scoped Defaults for Sessions and Messages
2	
3	## Status
4	
5	Accepted
6	
7	## Date
8	
9	2026-03-18
10	
11	## Context
12	
13	ADR-001 made `sessions` and `messages` progressively explorable by allowing all filters to be omitted. That improved discovery, but it also made the most common workflow noisier: users often run `mmr` from inside a project directory because they want the history for that project, not the combined history for every project on disk.
14	
15	The repository already had one cwd-aware behavior in `export`, which infers the project from the current working directory using the canonical path for Codex and the slash-to-hyphen form for Claude. `remember` also already defaults its project from cwd. `sessions` and `messages` were the remaining commands that still required an explicit `--project` to feel project-local.
16	
17	The change must preserve two edge cases explicitly:
18	
19	- If cwd auto-discovery fails, the CLI should not error; it should fall back to the previous global behavior.
20	- If cwd auto-discovery succeeds but that project has no messages, the CLI must return the empty result for that project instead of silently widening scope.
21	
22	The change also introduces env-driven defaults for source selection and remember agent selection, so the command-line defaults can be tuned without changing every invocation.
23	
24	## Decision
25	
26	### `sessions` and `messages` auto-discover the cwd project by default
27	
28	When the user omits `--project`, `sessions` and `messages` resolve the current working directory to a project identifier and scope the query to that project by default.
29	
30	- Codex matching uses the canonical cwd path.
31	- Claude matching uses the same project resolution machinery already used by `export` and project filtering.
32	- If cwd discovery fails, the command falls back to the previous global behavior.
33	- If cwd discovery succeeds but no history matches, the command returns an empty result.
34	
35	### `--all` disables the new default project scoping
36	
37	Both `sessions` and `messages` gain `--all`.
38	
39	- `--all` bypasses cwd project auto-discovery.
40	- `--all` does not change source behavior; `--source` and source defaults still apply.
41	- `--project` remains the explicit way to scope to a chosen project.
42	
43	### New environment variables can supply defaults
44	
45	- `MMR_AUTO_DISCOVER_PROJECT=0` disables cwd project auto-discovery for `sessions` and `messages`.
46	- `MMR_AUTO_DISCOVER_PROJECT=1` or unset keeps cwd project auto-discovery enabled.
47	- `MMR_DEFAULT_SOURCE=codex|claude` supplies the default source filter when `--source` is omitted.
48	- `MMR_DEFAULT_REMEMBER_AGENT=cursor|codex|gemini` supplies the default `remember --agent` value when `--agent` is omitted. When unset, the default backend is Cursor (`composer-2-fast` unless `--model` overrides).
49	
50	Empty or invalid values for `MMR_DEFAULT_SOURCE` and `MMR_DEFAULT_REMEMBER_AGENT` are treated as unset so the CLI remains usable.
51	
52	## Consequences
53	
54	- Running `mmr sessions` or `mmr messages` from inside a project directory is now project-local by default instead of global.
55	- Users can recover the historical global behavior with `--all` or `MMR_AUTO_DISCOVER_PROJECT=0`.
56	- The existing JSON response shapes do not change; only the default filters do.
57	- Repository guidance and contract tests must be updated to prevent stale assumptions that unfiltered `sessions` or `messages` are always global.
58

I now have the load-bearing facts. The most important discovery contradicts the framing in the problem statement, and it's central to the design, so let me state it before I fan out:

**`mmr` has no notion of the "current session."** The default `mmr messages` (no flags) scopes to the current *project* via cwd auto-discovery and returns the newest 50 messages across *all* sessions in that project, ordered chronologically (`src/cli.rs:807`, `src/messages/service.rs:298`). There is no environment-variable or other detection of "the session the agent is in right now." The only session-aware shortcut today is `--latest [N]`, which picks the single newest session and returns its newest N messages. So "previous session" can only be defined relative to recency ordering, and the live session may or may not even be ingested yet (your global rules already note `mmr` can lag the newest sessions). That freshness gap is the crux of the whole design.

The other load-bearing facts: message-level slicing already exists as `--from-message-index`/`--to-message-index` (zero-based, from-inclusive, to-exclusive, `usize` so no negatives), which creates a naming-collision risk with a *session*-level index axis. Output is machine-readable JSON across five providers (claude/codex/cursor/grok/pi).

# Reverse Session Selection for `mmr messages` — Recommended Design

## 1. Recommendation

Ship a single new session-axis flag, **`--session-back <N>`** (an unsigned, one-based "sessions ago" ordinal), plus the **`mmr prev [N]`** subcommand as ergonomic sugar, and surface a new optional **`session_selection`** block in the JSON response. The mental model is one sentence: **sessions in the current scope are a stack ordered newest-first, and `--session-back 1` means "the session before the newest one mmr can see," with no zero and no negatives because mmr cannot know which session you are running inside.** This is primarily Lens B and Lens D fused: B's spoken-ordinal model and `prev` subcommand for first-try guessability, D's "age 0 is the presumed-live session and is not selectable by default" safety rule, and the self-describing response and loud out-of-range errors that both contributed. I am explicitly overriding the maintainer's `--from-index -1 / --to-index -1` strawman in three ways (negative axis, the `index` noun, and inclusive-both-ends ranges) and explain each override below.

## 2. The Core Decision: What "Current" Means

**There is no "current."** I verified in `src/` that mmr has no live-session detection: there is no `CLAUDE_SESSION_ID`, `CODEX_SESSION`, or any runtime mechanism, and the only `current_session_id` symbols (`src/capture.rs`) are local variables that parse provider JSONL during ingest, not identifiers of the running agent. `latest_session_messages` in `src/messages/service.rs` keys purely off the single newest message. So "current" can only be defined by **recency**, never by identity, and any design that pretends otherwise will silently return wrong context.

**Chosen convention: a one-based, non-negative "sessions ago" ordinal counted from the newest session in scope, where the newest session (recency-age 0) is deliberately NOT addressable by default.**

- Recency-age 0 = the single newest session mmr can see (greatest `last_timestamp`), consistent with how `--latest` already behaves.
- `--session-back 1` = the session strictly older than the newest one = "the previous session."
- `--session-back 2` = two sessions older than the newest, and so on.

**Why age 0 is unreachable by default.** This is the crux. mmr reads provider JSONL files at invocation time and lags un-flushed writes. When an agent mid-conversation asks for "the previous session," the newest row in mmr's view may BE its own partially-flushed live session. If "previous" mapped to "the newest visible session," the agent could silently get fragments of its own transcript — the worst failure mode for a context tool. By making age 0 unselectable and defining "previous" as age 1, the common race cannot poison the result: whether or not the live session has flushed, `--session-back 1` skips the newest row. An explicit opt-in (`--include-newest`) makes age 0 reachable for callers who have verified it is safe.

**Resolving the strawman off-by-one.** The maintainer's `--from-index -1 = previous` is only correct if the current session is excluded from the axis, which directly contradicts naive "newest = -1" indexing. The strawman never says which. My scheme removes the contradiction by construction: "previous" is unambiguously `--session-back 1`; there is no spelling where current and previous collide on one index. I **override** the negative axis entirely (rationale in §3 and §9).

**Honesty about the residual risk.** Excluding age 0 defends against the race but cannot prove the agent's live session is the one that was skipped. So the response echoes a `skipped_newest` object (the held-back age-0 session's id, last timestamp, and recency hint) and an `assumed_live` flag, and ranking reuses the exact `sort_sessions` tie-break chain so the result is deterministic. The agent, which knows its own wall-clock start, can compare and self-correct in one read with no extra round-trip.

## 3. Flag / Command Spec

Two axes, never confusable, because the session axis never uses the word **index** and the message axis never uses the word **session** or **back**.

### New session axis

| Flag / command | Type | Default | Behavior |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--session-back <N>` | `u32`, N ≥ 1 | none | Select the single session at recency-age N, counting back from (and excluding) the newest session in scope. `--session-back 1` = previous session. Value `0` is rejected with a structured error unless `--include-newest` is set. |
| `--session-range <FROM..TO>` | string, two `u32` ages, `FROM ≥ TO ≥ 1` | none | Select a contiguous span of sessions by recency-age, both ends **inclusive**, written older-bound `..` newer-bound so it reads left-to-right oldest-to-newest. `2..1` = the two sessions before the newest (ages 1 and 2). |
| `--include-newest` | bool flag | `false` | Opt-in escape hatch that makes age 0 addressable, so `--session-back 0` and ranges touching 0 become legal. Off by default so the dangerous case is never the default. |
| `mmr prev [N]` | subcommand, `N` = `u32` ≥ 1, default 1 | `prev` = age 1 | Sugar for `mmr messages --session-back N`. `mmr prev` = the previous session; `mmr prev 2` = two sessions back. Accepts the scope flags (`--project`, `--all`, `--source`, `--limit`, `--pretty`). The guess-it-first-try entry point. |

## 4. Worked Examples

| Command | Result |
|---|---|
| `mmr prev` | Messages of the previous session (recency-age 1) in the cwd project, chronological, capped at `--limit`. The maintainer's `--from-index -1 --to-index -1` maps here. Response carries `skipped_newest` (the age-0 session held back) so the agent can confirm it was not its own live transcript. |
| `mmr messages --session-back 1 --pretty` | Identical session to `mmr prev`, pretty-printed. `session_selection.selected[0]` reports `{age:1, session_id, source, project_name, last_timestamp, message_count}`. |
| `mmr messages --session-range 2..1` | The two sessions before the newest (ages 1 and 2) in the cwd project. | `mmr messages --session-range 2..1 --all` | **Cross-source / cross-project case.** Same two-session span computed over ALL projects and all sources, ranked by the deterministic `sort_sessions` chain (last_timestamp, then message_count, session_id, project_name, project_path, source). | `mmr messages --session-back 0 --include-newest` | The newest session in scope (age 0), full session body. The explicit opt-in for "I have verified it is safe to read the newest row." |
| `mmr messages --session-back 5` (only 3 older sessions in scope) | Rejected: `{error:"session_back_out_of_range", message:"requested session age 5 but only 3 older sessions are in scope", total_sessions_in_scope:4, max_selectable_age:3}`. No silent clamp to the oldest, no silent empty result. |

## 5. Response (JSON) Changes

Add exactly **one** optional field to `ApiMessagesResponse`, serialized with `#[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]` so every existing response is byte-identical (the same convention already used for `next_command` at `src/types/api.rs:65`).

```jsonc
"session_selection": {
  "scope":       { "project": "mmr" | null, "all": false, "source": "codex" | null },
  "axis":        "session-back" | "session-range",
  "total_sessions_in_scope": 12,
  "selected": [
    {
      "age": 1,
      "session_id": "…",
      "source": "codex",
      "project_name": "mmr",
      "first_timestamp": "…",
      "last_timestamp": "…",
      "message_count": 87,
      "equivalent_command": "mmr messages --session <id>"
    }
  ],
  "skipped_newest": {
    "age": 0,
    "session_id": "…",
    "last_timestamp": "…",
    "assumed_live": true
  }
}
```

**Why each piece exists.** Session selection is invisible in the flat `messages[]` array (messages from multiple sessions interleave by timestamp), so without this block an agent cannot confirm which session it got. `selected` (with `source` + `project_name`) lets it verify intent and detect provider-mixing under `--all`. `total_sessions_in_scope` lets it validate `--session-back N` before asking. `skipped_newest` names the session held back so the agent understands why "previous" is age 1 and can detect the live-session race. `equivalent_command` gives a stable re-fetch handle by concrete id, which matters for pagination (see §8). 

**Critically, existing fields are not redefined.** `total_messages` keeps its current meaning — the scoped message count of the selected sessions, computed before the message-index window (`service.rs:320`). `next_page/next_offset/next_command` stay driven by the same `--limit/--offset` machinery. No contract test that snapshots a non-session-axis response can break.

## 6. Ergonomics Wins Beyond the Ask

1. **`mmr prev` — a one-token answer (from Lens B).** The literal command a human or agent types for "the previous session" works, and `mmr prev 2` extends naturally. The dominant in-session use case needs no flags and no mental arithmetic.
2. **Self-describing output (from Lenses C and D).** `session_selection` turns a recency-relative request into a verifiable result: the agent reads ids, ages, timestamps, and counts instead of trusting an index it cannot see help text for. `skipped_newest.assumed_live` makes the live-session race observable.
3. **Fail loud, never silently wrong (from Lens D and the edge analysis).** Out-of-range `--session-back` and selecting age 0 both return structured errors with nonzero exit, naming the available count, rather than clamping to the newest or returning empty. This is the single most important property for an automation-facing tool.
4. **Lexical axis separation (from Lenses A and C).** Every session-axis flag contains `session`/`back`; the message axis keeps `index`. `specs/messages.md` gets a one-line two-axis table. The "which index axis?" hazard is structurally impossible to hit.

## 7. Backward Compatibility & Migration

**Stays:** every existing flag (`--session`, `--project`, `--all`, `--latest`, `--from-message-index`, `--to-message-index`, `--limit`, `--offset`, `--sort-by`, `--order`) keeps identical behavior, type, and default. `mmr messages` with no new flag returns the exact current JSON, because `session_selection` is `Option` + skip-if-none. The message-index axis and its `specs/messages.md` contract are untouched. ADR-002 cwd-scoped defaults still govern scope.

**Added:** `--session-back`, `--session-range`, `--include-newest`, the `mmr prev` subcommand, and the optional `session_selection` field — all purely additive.

**Contract-test impact:** none for existing assertions. The contract tests in `tests/cli_contract.rs` that snapshot default `mmr messages` output stay green because the new field is omitted when no session-axis flag is used. New tests are added for the new paths (see §8). The strawman spelling is not shipped, so there is no negative-index contract to honor or later regret; anyone copy-pasting `--from-index -1` gets a clean clap "unexpected argument" error, and the docs present `mmr prev` / `--session-back 1` as the direct replacement.

## 8. Phased Implementation Plan

**Phase 1 — Pure parsers and validators (lowest risk, test-first).** Add `parse_session_range("FROM..TO") -> RangeInclusive<u32>` and the `--session-back` validator in `src/cli.rs`, mirroring the existing `validate_message_index_range` pattern. Table-test: `1` → age 1, `2..1` → ages {1,2}, `0` rejected, `-1` rejected, reversed range rejected. No production behavior yet.

**Phase 2 — Service selection method (`src/messages/service.rs`).** Add `messages_by_session_age(scope, source_filter, ages: RangeInclusive<u32>, include_newest, options)` that ranks in-scope `SessionAggregate`s with the existing `sort_sessions(Timestamp, Desc)`, assigns ages (0 = newest), validates age-0/out-of-range, resolves to concrete session keys, then reuses the existing per-session filter + `sort_messages` + `apply_message_index_range` + newest-window pagination block (`service.rs:298-360`) verbatim. Inject `now()` for deterministic tests. Pin exact `session_id`s for ages 0, 1, and a 2-wide range against a fixture, the way `latest_session_messages` ordering is already pinned.

**Phase 3 — Wiring and response (`src/cli.rs`, `src/types/api.rs`).** Add the four clap surfaces and the mutual-exclusion guard beside the existing `validate_message_index_range` call; add the `SessionSelection`/`SelectedSession`/`SkippedNewest` structs and the optional field on `ApiMessagesResponse`; route `mmr prev` into the messages path.

**Phase 4 (RISKIEST) — Pagination round-trip (`build_next_messages_command`, ~`cli.rs:3681`).** Recency-relative ages are unstable across time: if a new session lands between calls, `--session-back 1` points somewhere new. So `next_command` for a paged session-axis query **must not echo `--session-back`** — it must pin to the resolved concrete session id(s) via the `equivalent_command` form, freezing the session set while `--offset` walks the merged message stream. Single-session pages cleanly by `--session <id>`; multi-session ranges need the session set frozen in the emitted command. Write the failing pagination-stability test first; this is the main correctness risk.

**Phase 5 — Docs.** Add `adrs/003-reverse-session-selection.md` recording the recency-not-identity definition, the age-0-excluded default, and the three strawman overrides. Add a `--session-back` / `mmr prev` section to `specs/messages.md` with the two-axis table and the live-session-lag caveat. Add a row to `docs/references/session-lookup-invariants.md` noting session-axis selectors stay cwd-scoped while literal `--session <id>` stays global.

## 9. Open Questions for the Maintainer

1. **Range syntax: ship `--session-range` now, or `--session-back` only in v1?** `--session-back 1` covers the dominant "previous session" case alone, and the strawman's two-session case is rare. **Recommended default: ship both,** since `--session-range` is cheap once the ranking exists, but if scope must shrink, drop `--session-range` and keep `--session-back` + `prev`.

2. **Age-0 default: unreachable (recommended) vs reachable?** Excluding age 0 by default is the safety stance that defends against the flush-lag race; the cost is surprising anyone who expected "newest = previous." **Recommended default: keep age 0 unreachable, with `--include-newest` as the opt-in,** and lean on `skipped_newest` in the response to teach the model.

3. **Out-of-range: hard error (recommended) vs empty result?** The existing message-index code returns empty when `end < start`, but silently returning zero messages for a session-axis typo hides the caller's mistake. **Recommended default: hard structured error** naming the available count, distinct from a legitimately empty scope (which returns an empty response with `total_sessions_in_scope: 0` per ADR-002).

4. **`--all` cross-project ranking: index the merged all-projects deck (recommended) vs require a project for the session axis?** Under cwd defaults the deck is one project and ages are intuitive; under `--all` a single age can cross project and provider boundaries. **Recommended default: allow it and make it self-explaining** via `source` + `project_name` in every `selected` entry, with an explicit doc note.

5. **Default `--source` scope: should the session axis honor `MMR_DEFAULT_SOURCE`?** When the caller says "my previous session," landing on a different provider's session that happens to be newer is rarely intended. **Recommended default: yes — the session axis respects `--source`/`MMR_DEFAULT_SOURCE` exactly as `--latest` does today,** so an unset source mixes providers (deterministically) and setting it narrows cleanly.

Here is the recommended strategy. The design panel produced four independent proposals plus an adversarial edge-case pass; what follows is the synthesized recommendation, with the parts I changed from your strawman called out explicitly.

## The reframe that drives everything

Your problem statement says `mmr` "defaults to the current session." It does not. `mmr messages` with no flags scopes to the current *project* (via working-directory auto-discovery) and returns the newest fifty messages across *all* sessions in that project. More importantly, `mmr` has no way at all to know which session the calling agent is running inside: there is no session-ID environment variable and no other runtime signal (I grepped the source to confirm). The only session-aware shortcut today is `--latest`, which picks the single newest session by timestamp.

This matters because "the previous session" can only be defined by recency, never by identity. And there is a race underneath it: `mmr` reads the provider log files at invocation time and lags writes that haven't flushed yet, so the newest session it can see might actually *be* your own half-written live session. If "previous" naively meant "the newest visible session," an agent could silently get fragments of its own transcript back. That is the worst possible failure for a context tool, and avoiding it is the spine of the design.

## Recommendation in one sentence

Add a single unsigned, one-based "sessions ago" flag, `--session-back <N>`, with `mmr prev [N]` as plain-language sugar and an optional `--session-range FROM..TO` for spans. The mental model: **sessions in scope are a stack ordered newest-first; `--session-back 1` is the session before the newest one `mmr` can see; there is no zero and no negative, because `mmr` cannot know which session you are in.**

## How "current" is resolved

Recency-age 0 is the single newest session in scope. It is **deliberately not selectable by default**, because it is the one most likely to be your own live session. `--session-back 1` is therefore "the previous session," and it skips the newest row whether or not your live session has flushed. An explicit `--include-newest` flag unlocks age 0 for callers who have verified it is safe. The response also echoes which session was held back (see the JSON section), so an agent can compare it against its own start time and detect the race in a single read rather than a second round-trip.

This also dissolves the off-by-one hiding in your strawman. `--from-index -1` meaning "previous" is only correct if the current session is excluded from the axis, which contradicts the naive reading where the newest item is -1. The strawman never says which it is. With `--session-back 1` there is no spelling where "current" and "previous" can collide.

## Flag and command specification

| Surface | Type | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| `--session-back <N>` | `u32`, N ≥ 1 | The single session at recency-age N, counting back from and excluding the newest. `1` = previous session. `0` rejected unless `--include-newest`. |
| `--session-range <FROM..TO>` | two `u32` ages, FROM ≥ TO ≥ 1 | A contiguous span by age, both ends inclusive. `2..1` = the two sessions before the newest. |
| `--include-newest` | bool | Opt-in that makes age 0 addressable. Off by default. |
| `mmr prev [N]` | subcommand, default N=1 | Sugar for `mmr messages --session-back N`. The guess-it-first-try entry point. |

The existing message-level flags `--from-message-index` (inclusive) and `--to-message-index` (exclusive) keep their exact names, `usize` types, and contract; they slice *within* the messages after a session is chosen. The two axes never share vocabulary: the session axis always says `session`/`back`, the message axis always says `index`. This is the deliberate fix for the naming-collision hazard your strawman's `--from-index`/`--to-index` would have created sitting one space away from `--from-message-index`.

Three places where I overrode your strawman, and why:

1. **Negative axis becomes an unsigned ordinal.** A bare `-1` is swallowed by the argument parser as an unknown flag unless special-cased, and a signed type would ripple into the existing pagination-command builder that formats `usize`. An unsigned ordinal sidesteps all of that and is unambiguous.
2. **`--from-index/--to-index` becomes `--session-back`/`--session-range`.** Removes the "which index am I touching?" confusion against the existing message-index flags.
3. **The inclusive-both-ends `-1 -1` range becomes a count plus a distinct `..` range.** The strawman's inclusive endpoints contradict the exclusive `--to-message-index` you already ship; a single `--session-back N` has no endpoint ambiguity at all.

The four session selectors (`--session-back`, `--session-range`, `--session <id>`, `--latest`) are mutually exclusive and error loudly if combined, exactly like the existing teleport "pass either --session or --latest" guard.

## Your two strawman commands, translated

| Your strawman | Recommended | 
|---|---|
| `mmr messages --from-index -1 --to-index -1` | `mmr prev` or `mmr messages --session-back 1` |
| `mmr messages --from-index -2 --to-index -1` | `mmr messages --session-range 2..1` |

## Self-describing JSON (the agent-UX win)

Because messages from multiple sessions interleave by timestamp in the flat `messages` array, an agent currently cannot tell which session it actually received. The design adds one optional `session_selection` block (omitted entirely when no session-axis flag is used, so existing output is unchanged). It reports the scope, the axis used, the total sessions in scope, and for each selected session its age, ID, source, project, timestamps, message count, and a stable `equivalent_command` to re-fetch it by concrete ID. It also reports `skipped_newest`: the held-back age-0 session, flagged `assumed_live`. That single field is what lets an agent confirm "previous" didn't accidentally mean "me."

Failures are loud, never silent: `--session-back 5` when only three older sessions exist returns a structured error naming the available count, rather than clamping or returning empty; selecting age 0 without the opt-in returns a structured error explaining the live-session risk.

## Implementation shape and the one risky step

The work is purely additive and breaks no existing contract test. It phases as: pure parsers/validators in `src/cli.rs` (test-first, mirroring the existing `validate_message_index_range`); a new selection method in `src/messages/service.rs` that ranks sessions with the existing `sort_sessions` comparator, assigns ages, and then reuses the existing per-session filter, sort, and pagination block verbatim; the clap wiring and the new response struct in `src/types/api.rs`; an ADR plus a `specs/messages.md` update.

The one genuinely risky step is pagination. Recency-relative ages are unstable over time, so if a new session lands between two paged calls, `--session-back 1` would point somewhere new. The `next_command` for a paged session-axis query must therefore pin to the resolved concrete session IDs, not echo `--session-back`, freezing the session set while `--offset` walks the merged stream. That needs a failing stability test written first.

## Decisions that are genuinely yours to make

Each has a recommended default; none blocks the others.

1. **Ship the range now, or just `--session-back` + `prev` in v1?** Recommended: ship both, since the range is cheap once ranking exists, but `--session-back` alone covers the dominant case if you want to keep v1 tiny.
2. **Keep age 0 unreachable by default?** Recommended: yes, with `--include-newest` as the opt-in. This is the safety stance against the flush-lag race.
3. **Out-of-range: hard error or empty result?** Recommended: hard error naming the available count, distinct from a legitimately empty scope.
4. **Under `--all`, index the merged all-projects deck or require a project?** Recommended: allow it and make it self-explaining via `source` + `project_name` on every selected entry.
5. **Does the session axis honor `--source`/`MMR_DEFAULT_SOURCE`?** Recommended: yes, exactly as `--latest` does, so you don't land on a different provider's newer session by accident.

If you'd like, I can lock these defaults and implement Phase 1 and 2 (the parsers, validators, and the service method with fixture-pinned tests) so you can see the behavior before committing to the clap surface and the response schema. Or if any of the five decisions should go a different way, tell me and I'll fold it in first.
